theorem mathd_algebra_59 :
  ∀ (b : ℝ) (F : ℝ → ℝ → ℝ → ℝ → ℝ) (h₀: F = fun a b c d ↦ a ^ b + c ^ d) (h₁ : F 4 b 2 3 = 12), b = 1 := by
  intro b F h₀ h₁
  have h₂ : F 4 b 2 3 = (4 : ℝ) ^ b + (2 : ℝ) ^ (3 : ℝ) := by
    simp [h₀, Real.rpow_def_of_pos, Real.rpow_def_of_nonneg, Real.log_mul, Real.log_rpow, Real.log_pow]
    <;> ring_nf at *
    <;> norm_num
    <;> field_simp [Real.log_mul, Real.log_rpow, Real.log_pow]
    <;> ring_nf at *
    <;> norm_num
    <;> linarith
  rw [h₂] at h₁
  have h₃ : (4 : ℝ) ^ b + (2 : ℝ) ^ (3 : ℝ) = 12 := by linarith
  have h₄ : (4 : ℝ) ^ b = 12 - (2 : ℝ) ^ (3 : ℝ) := by linarith
  have h₅ : (4 : ℝ) ^ b = 12 - 8 := by
    norm_num at h₄ ⊢
    <;> linarith
  have h₆ : (4 : ℝ) ^ b = 4 := by
    norm_num at h₅ ⊢
    <;> linarith
  have h₇ : b = 1 := by
    -- We need to show that b = 1. We know that 4^b = 4.
    have h₈ : (4 : ℝ) ^ b = 4 := by linarith
    -- Take the natural logarithm of both sides.
    have h₉ : Real.log ((4 : ℝ) ^ b) = Real.log 4 := by rw [h₈]
    -- Use the logarithm power rule.
    have h₁₀ : b * Real.log 4 = Real.log 4 := by
      rw [Real.log_rpow (by norm_num : (4 : ℝ) > 0)] at h₉
      <;> linarith
    -- Divide both sides by log 4 (which is positive).
    have h₁₁ : b = 1 := by
      apply mul_left_cancel₀ (show (Real.log 4 : ℝ) ≠ 0 by
        -- Prove that log 4 ≠ 0.
        have h₁₂ : Real.log 4 > 0 := Real.log_pos (by norm_num)
        linarith)
      linarith
    exact h₁₁
  exact h₇

    simp [h₀]
    <;> norm_cast
    <;> simp_all
    <;> norm_num
    <;> ring_nf
    <;> norm_num
    <;> linarith
  
  rw [h₂] at h₁
  have h₃ : (4 : ℝ) ^ b + (2 : ℝ) ^ (3 : ℝ) = 12 := by linarith
  have h₄ : (4 : ℝ) ^ b = 12 - (2 : ℝ) ^ (3 : ℝ) := by linarith
  have h₅ : (4 : ℝ) ^ b = 8 := by
    norm_num at h₄ ⊢
    <;>
    linarith
  have h₆ : b = 1 := by
    -- We use the fact that 4^b = 8 to find b
    have h₇ : (4 : ℝ) ^ b = 8 := by linarith
    have h₈ : Real.log ((4 : ℝ) ^ b) = Real.log 8 := by rw [h₇]
    have h₉ : b * Real.log 4 = Real.log 8 := by
      have h₁₀ : Real.log ((4 : ℝ) ^ b) = b * Real.log 4 := by
        rw [Real.log_rpow (by norm_num : (4 : ℝ) > 0)]
        <;> ring_nf
      rw [h₁₀] at h₈
      exact h₈
    have h₁₀ : b = 1 := by
      have h₁₁ : Real.log 8 = 3 * Real.log 2 := by
        have h₁₂ : Real.log 8 = Real.log (2 ^ 3) := by norm_num
        rw [h₁₂]
        have h₁₃ : Real.log (2 ^ 3) = 3 * Real.log 2 := by
          rw [Real.log_pow]
          <;> norm_num
        rw [h₁₃]
        <;> ring_nf
      have h₁₂ : Real.log 4 = 2 * Real.log 2 := by
        have h₁₃ : Real.log 4 = Real.log (2 ^ 2) := by norm_num
        rw [h₁₃]
        have h₁₄ : Real.log (2 ^ 2) = 2 * Real.log 2 := by
          rw [Real.log_pow]
          <;> norm_num
        rw [h₁₄]
        <;> ring_nf
      rw [h₁₁] at h₉
      rw [h₁₂] at h₉
      have h₁₃ : b * (2 * Real.log 2) = 3 * Real.log 2 := by linarith
      have h₁₄ : Real.log 2 > 0 := Real.log_pos (by norm_num)
      have h₁₅ : b * 2 * Real.log 2 = 3 * Real.log 2 := by linarith
      have h₁₆ : b * 2 = 3 := by
        apply mul_left_cancel₀ (show (Real.log 2 : ℝ) ≠ 0 by linarith)
        linarith
      have h₁₇ : b = 1.5 := by
        norm_num at h₁₆ ⊢
        <;> nlinarith
      norm_num at h₁₇ ⊢
      <;> nlinarith
    exact h₁₀
  exact h₆
theorem mathd_algebra_59 :

  have h₄ : b = 1 := by

        -- Use the logarithm power rule

            -- Use the fact that 4 > 1 and log is increasing

          linarith
        -- Solve for b
        have h₁₁ : b = 1 := by
          apply mul_left_cancel₀ (show (Real.log 4 : ℝ) ≠ 0 by linarith)
          linarith
        exact h₁₁
      exact h₉
    exact h₆
  exact h₄

  exact h₆

  intro b F h₀ h₁
  have h₂ := h₁
  rw [h₀] at h₂
  -- Simplify the expression 4 ^ b + 2 ^ 3 = 12
  have h₃ : (4 : ℝ) ^ b + (2 : ℝ) ^ (3 : ℝ) = 12 := by
    norm_num [h₀] at h₂ ⊢
    <;>
    (try ring_nf at h₂ ⊢) <;>
    (try norm_num at h₂ ⊢) <;>
    (try linarith) <;>
    (try assumption) <;>
    (try
      {
        simp_all [Real.rpow_def_of_pos, Real.rpow_def_of_nonneg, Real.log_mul, Real.log_pow,
          Real.log_inv, Real.log_div, Real.log_one, Real.log_zero]
        <;>
        ring_nf at * <;>
        norm_num at * <;>
        nlinarith [Real.log_pos (by norm_num : (1 : ℝ) < 4), Real.log_pos (by norm_num : (1 : ℝ) < 2)]
      })
    <;>
    (try
      {
        nlinarith [Real.log_pos (by norm_num : (1 : ℝ) < 4), Real.log_pos (by norm_num : (1 : ℝ) < 2)]
      })
    <;>
    (try
      {
        linarith [Real.log_pos (by norm_num : (1 : ℝ) < 4), Real.log_pos (by norm_num : (1 : ℝ) < 2)]
      })
  -- Solve for b using the simplified equation
  have h₄ : b = 1 := by
    -- Use the fact that 2 ^ 3 = 8 to simplify the equation to 4 ^ b = 4
    have h₅ : (2 : ℝ) ^ (3 : ℝ) = 8 := by norm_num
    rw [h₅] at h₃
    have h₆ : (4 : ℝ) ^ b = 4 := by linarith
    -- Solve for b using logarithms
    have h₇ : Real.log ((4 : ℝ) ^ b) = Real.log 4 := by rw [h₆]
    have h₈ : b * Real.log 4 = Real.log 4 := by
      rw [Real.log_rpow (by norm_num : (4 : ℝ) > 0)] at h₇
      <;> linarith
    have h₉ : Real.log 4 > 0 := Real.log_pos (by norm_num)
    have h₁₀ : b = 1 := by
      apply mul_left_cancel₀ (show (Real.log 4 : ℝ) ≠ 0 by linarith)
      linarith
    exact h₁₀
  exact h₄

    have h₁₀ : Real.log 4 > 0 := Real.log_pos (by norm_num)
    have h₁₁ : b = 1 := by

        rw [h₁₀]

theorem mathd_algebra_59 :
  ∀ (b : ℝ) (F : ℝ → ℝ → ℝ → ℝ → ℝ) (h₀: F = fun a b c d ↦ a ^ b + c ^ d) (h₁ : F 4 b 2 3 = 12), b = 1 := by
  intro b F h₀ h₁
  have h₂ := h₁
  rw [h₀] at h₂
  -- Simplify the expression inside the hypothesis
  have h₃ : (4 : ℝ) ^ b + (2 : ℝ) ^ (3 : ℝ) = 12 := by
    norm_num at h₂ ⊢
    <;>
    (try ring_nf at h₂ ⊢) <;>
    (try simp_all [Real.rpow_def_of_pos, Real.rpow_def_of_nonneg]) <;>
    (try ring_nf at h₂ ⊢) <;>
    (try norm_num at h₂ ⊢) <;>
    (try linarith)
    <;>
    (try
      {
        simp_all [Real.rpow_def_of_pos, Real.rpow_def_of_nonneg]
        <;>
        ring_nf at *
        <;>
        norm_num at *
        <;>
        nlinarith [Real.rpow_pos_of_pos (by norm_num : (0 : ℝ) < 2) 3]
      })
    <;>
    (try
      {
        simp_all [Real.rpow_def_of_pos, Real.rpow_def_of_nonneg]
        <;>
        ring_nf at *
        <;>
        norm_num at *
        <;>
        nlinarith [Real.rpow_pos_of_pos (by norm_num : (0 : ℝ) < 2) 3]
      })
    <;>
    (try
      {
        simp_all [Real.rpow_def_of_pos, Real.rpow_def_of_nonneg]
        <;>
        ring_nf at *
        <;>
        norm_num at *
        <;>
        nlinarith [Real.rpow_pos_of_pos (by norm_num : (0 : ℝ) < 2) 3]
      })
  -- Solve for b
  have h₄ : (4 : ℝ) ^ b = 8 := by
    norm_num at h₃ ⊢
    <;> linarith
  have h₅ : b = 1 := by
    have h₆ : (4 : ℝ) ^ b = 8 := h₄
    have h₇ : b = 1 := by
      -- Use the fact that 4^b = 8 to solve for b
      have h₈ : Real.log ((4 : ℝ) ^ b) = Real.log 8 := by rw [h₆]
      have h₉ : b * Real.log 4 = Real.log 8 := by
        rw [Real.log_rpow (by norm_num : (4 : ℝ) > 0)] at h₈
        exact h₈
      have h₁₀ : b * Real.log 4 = Real.log 8 := h₉
      have h₁₁ : b = 1 := by
        -- Solve for b using the properties of logarithms
        have h₁₂ : Real.log 8 = Real.log (2 ^ 3) := by norm_num
        have h₁₃ : Real.log (2 ^ 3) = 3 * Real.log 2 := by
          rw [Real.log_pow]
          <;> norm_num
        have h₁₄ : Real.log 4 = Real.log (2 ^ 2) := by norm_num
        have h₁₅ : Real.log (2 ^ 2) = 2 * Real.log 2 := by
          rw [Real.log_pow]
          <;> norm_num
        have h₁₆ : b * Real.log 4 = Real.log 8 := h₁₀
        have h₁₇ : b * (2 * Real.log 2) = 3 * Real.log 2 := by
          rw [h₁₄, h₁₂, h₁₃] at h₁₆
          ring_nf at h₁₆ ⊢
          <;> linarith
        have h₁₈ : b = 1 := by
          have h₁₉ : Real.log 2 > 0 := Real.log_pos (by norm_num)
          have h₂₀ : 2 * Real.log 2 ≠ 0 := by positivity
          have h₂₁ : b * (2 * Real.log 2) = 3 * Real.log 2 := h₁₇
          have h₂₂ : b * (2 * Real.log 2) = b * 2 * Real.log 2 := by ring
          rw [h₂₂] at h₂₁
          have h₂₃ : b * 2 * Real.log 2 = 3 * Real.log 2 := h₂₁
          have h₂₄ : b * 2 = 3 := by
            apply mul_left_cancel₀ (show (Real.log 2 : ℝ) ≠ 0 by positivity)
            linarith
          nlinarith
        exact h₁₈
      exact h₁₁
    exact h₇
  exact h₅

  have h₁ : 0 ∈ { y : ℝ | ∃ a b : ℝ, 1 < b ∧ b ≤ a ∧ y = Real.logb a (a / b) + Real.logb b (b / a) } := by
    /-

    Let's choose \( a = 2 \) and \( b = 1.5 \).
    
    First, verify \( 1 < b \):
    \[ 1 < 1.5 \]
    which is true.
    
    Next, verify \( b \leq a \):
    \[ 1.5 \leq 2 \]
    which is true.
    
    Now, we need to check that \( 0 = \log_a \left( \frac{a}{b} \right) + \log_b \left( \frac{b}{a} \right) \).
    
    Calculate \( \log_a \left( \frac{a}{b} \right) \):
    \[ \log_a \left( \frac{a}{b} \right) = \log_a a - \log_a b = 1 - \log_a b \]
    
    Calculate \( \log_b \left( \frac{b}{a} \right) \):
    \[ \log_b \left( \frac{b}{a} \right) = \log_b b - \log_b a = 1 - \log_b a \]
    
    Thus, the sum is:
    \[ \log_a \left( \frac{a}{b} \right) + \log_b \left( \frac{b}{a} \right) = (1 - \log_a b) + (1 - \log_b a) = 2 - (\log_a b + \log_b a) \]
    
    We need this sum to be \( 0 \):
    \[ 2 - (\log_a b + \log_b a) = 0 \]
    \[ \log_a b + \log_b a = 2 \]
    
    Using the change of base formula, \( \log_a b = \frac{\ln b}{\ln a} \) and \( \log_b a = \frac{\ln a}{\ln b} \), so:
    \[ \frac{\ln b}{\ln a} + \frac{\ln a}{\ln b} = 2 \]
    
    Let \( x = \ln a \) and \( y = \ln b \). Then:
    \[ \frac{y}{x} + \frac{x}{y} = 2 \]
    \[ \frac{y^2 + x^2}{xy} = 2 \]
    \[ y^2 + x^2 = 2xy \]
    \[ y^2 - 2xy + x^2 = 0 \]
    \[ (y - x)^2 = 0 \]
    \[ y = x \]

    which is true.
    
    Next, verify \( b \leq a \):

    Now, we need to check that \( 0 = \log_a \left( \frac{a}{b} \right) + \log_b \left( \frac{b}{a} \right) \).

    \[ \log_a \left( \frac{a}{b} \right) = \log_a a - \log_a b = 1 - \log_a b \]
    
    Calculate \( \log_b \left( \frac{b}{a} \right) \):
    \[ \log_b \left( \frac{b}{a} \right) = \log_b b - \log_b a = 1 - \log_b a \]
    
    Thus, the sum is:
    \[ \log_a \left( \frac{a}{b} \right) + \log_b \left( \frac{b}{a} \right) = (1 - \log_a b) + (1 - \log_b a) = 2 - (\log_a b + \log_b a) \]
    
    We need this sum to be \( 0 \):

    \[ \frac{\ln b}{\ln a} + \frac{\ln a}{\ln b} = 2 \]
    
    Let \( x = \ln a \) and \( y = \ln b \). Then:
    \[ \frac{y}{x} + \frac{x}{y} = 2 \]

    Therefore, if \( a = b \), the condition is satisfied. However, in our initial choice, \( a = 1.5 \) and \( b = 1.5 \), so \( a = b \). Thus, the condition is satisfied.

theorem amc12a_2003_p24 :
  IsGreatest { y : ℝ | ∃ a b : ℝ, 1 < b ∧ b ≤ a ∧ y = Real.logb a (a / b) + Real.logb b (b / a) } 0 := by

    /-
    We need to show that 0 is in the set \(\{ y : \mathbb{R} \mid \exists a, b \in \mathbb{R}, 1 < b \land b \leq a \land y = \log_b a (a / b) + \log_b a (b / a) \}\). This means we need to find real numbers \(a\) and \(b\) such that \(1 < b\), \(b \leq a\), and \(0 = \log_b a (a / b) + \log_b a (b / a)\).
    
    Let's choose \(a = 2\) and \(b = 2\). Then \(1 < b\) is satisfied because \(1 < 2\). Next, \(b \leq a\) is satisfied because \(2 \leq 2\). Now, we need to verify that \(0 = \log_b a (a / b) + \log_b a (b / a)\).
    
    First, compute \(\log_b a (a / b)\):
    \[
    \log_b a (a / b) = \frac{\ln (a / b)}{\ln b} = \frac{\ln (2 / 2)}{\ln 2} = \frac{\ln 1}{\ln 2} = \frac{0}{\ln 2} = 0.
    \]
    
    Next, compute \(\log_b a (b / a)\):
    \[
    \log_b a (b / a) = \frac{\ln (b / a)}{\ln b} = \frac{\ln (2 / 2)}{\ln 2} = \frac{\ln 1}{\ln 2} = \frac{0}{\ln 2} = 0.
    \]
    
    Adding these two results gives:
    \[
    \log_b a (a / b) + \log_b a (b / a) = 0 + 0 = 0.
    \]
    
    Thus, \(0\) is in the set.
    -/

        have h₄ : Real.log (2 / 2) = 0 := by

        rw [h₄]

        rw [h₅]
        <;> simp
        <;> norm_num
      have h₄ : Real.logb 2 (2 / 2) + Real.logb 2 (2 / 2) = 0 := by
        linarith
      simpa [h₂, h₃, h₄] using h₄
  have h₂ : ∀ y ∈ { y : ℝ | ∃ a b : ℝ, 1 < b ∧ b ≤ a ∧ y = Real.logb a (a / b) + Real.logb b (b / a) }, y ≤ 0 := by

theorem amc12a_2003_p24 :
  IsGreatest { y : ℝ | ∃ a b : ℝ, 1 < b ∧ b ≤ a ∧ y = Real.logb a (a / b) + Real.logb b (b / a) } 0 := by
  have h₀ : 0 ∈ { y : ℝ | ∃ a b : ℝ, 1 < b ∧ b ≤ a ∧ y = Real.logb a (a / b) + Real.logb b (b / a) } := by
    /-
    We need to find \( a \) and \( b \) such that \( 1 < b \), \( b \leq a \), and \( y = \log_a \left( \frac{a}{b} \right) + \log_b \left( \frac{b}{a} \right) = 0 \).
    
    Given the problem, we choose \( a = 2 \) and \( b = 2 \). However, this choice does not satisfy \( 1 < b \) because \( b = 2 \) is not greater than 1. 
    
    Next, we choose \( a = 3 \) and \( b = 2 \). Here, \( 1 < b = 2 \) and \( b = 2 \leq a = 3 \). We then compute:
    \[
    y = \log_3 \left( \frac{3}{2} \right) + \log_2 \left( \frac{2}{3} \right)
    \]
    Simplifying, we get:
    \[
    y = \log_3 \left( \frac{3}{2} \right) + \log_2 \left( \frac{2}{3} \right) = \log_3 3 - \log_3 2 + \log_2 2 - \log_2 3 = 1 - \log_3 2 + 1 - \log_2 3
    \]
    However, this does not simplify to 0. 
    
    We need to find another pair \( (a, b) \) that satisfies the conditions and makes \( y = 0 \). 
    
    Consider \( a = 2 \) and \( b = \sqrt{2} \). Here, \( 1 < b = \sqrt{2} \approx 1.414 \) and \( b = \sqrt{2} \leq a = 2 \). We then compute:
    \[
    y = \log_2 \left( \frac{2}{\sqrt{2}} \right) + \log_{\sqrt{2}} \left( \frac{\sqrt{2}}{2} \right)
    \]
    Simplifying, we get:
    \[
    y = \log_2 \left( \frac{2}{\sqrt{2}} \right) + \log_{\sqrt{2}} \left( \frac{\sqrt{2}}{2} \right) = \log_2 2 - \log_2 \sqrt{2} + \log_{\sqrt{2}} \sqrt{2} - \log_{\sqrt{2}} 2 = 1 - \frac{1}{2} + 1 - 2 = 0
    \]
    This choice of \( a \) and \( b \) satisfies the conditions and makes \( y = 0 \).
    -/
    use 2, Real.sqrt 2
    have h₁ : (1 : ℝ) < Real.sqrt 2 := by
      norm_num [Real.lt_sqrt, Real.sqrt_lt]
    have h₂ : Real.sqrt 2 ≤ (2 : ℝ) := by
      norm_num [Real.sqrt_le_iff]
    have h₃ : Real.logb 2 (2 / Real.sqrt 2) + Real.logb (Real.sqrt 2) (Real.sqrt 2 / 2) = 0 := by
      have h₄ : Real.logb 2 (2 / Real.sqrt 2) = Real.logb 2 2 - Real.logb 2 (Real.sqrt 2) := by
        rw [Real.logb_div (by positivity) (by positivity)]
        <;> ring_nf
        <;> field_simp [Real.logb, Real.log_div, Real.log_mul, Real.log_rpow, Real.log_sqrt, Real.log_pow]
        <;> ring_nf
        <;> norm_num
        <;> linarith [Real.log_pos (by norm_num : (1 : ℝ) < 2)]
      have h₅ : Real.logb (Real.sqrt 2) (Real.sqrt 2 / 2) = Real.logb (Real.sqrt 2) (Real.sqrt 2) - Real.logb (Real.sqrt 2) 2 := by
        rw [Real.logb_div (by positivity) (by positivity)]
        <;> ring_nf
        <;> field_simp [Real.logb, Real.log_div, Real.log_mul, Real.log_rpow, Real.log_sqrt, Real.log_pow]
        <;> ring_nf
        <;> norm_num
        <;> linarith [Real.log_pos (by norm_num : (1 : ℝ) < 2)]
      have h₆ : Real.logb 2 2 = 1 := by
        norm_num [Real.logb_eq_iff_rpow_eq]
      have h₇ : Real.logb (Real.sqrt 2) (Real.sqrt 2) = 1 := by
        norm_num [Real.logb_eq_iff_rpow_eq]
      have h₈ : Real.logb 2 (Real.sqrt 2) = Real.log (Real.sqrt 2) / Real.log 2 := by
        rw [Real.logb]
        <;> ring_nf
      have h₉ : Real.logb (Real.sqrt 2) 2 = Real.log 2 / Real.log (Real.sqrt 2) := by
        rw [Real.logb]
        <;> ring_nf
      have h₁₀ : Real.log (Real.sqrt 2) = Real.log 2 / 2 := by
        have h₁₀₁ : Real.log (Real.sqrt 2) = Real.log 2 / 2 := by
          rw [Real.log_sqrt (by positivity)]
          <;> ring_nf
        rw [h₁₀₁]
      rw [h₄, h₅, h₆, h₇, h₈, h₉, h₁₀]
      <;> field_simp [Real.log_mul, Real.log_div, Real.log_rpow, Real.log_sqrt, Real.log_pow]
      <;> ring_nf
      <;> norm_num

  IsGreatest { y : ℝ | ∃ a b : ℝ, 1 < b ∧ b ≤ a ∧ y = Real.logb a (a / b) + Real.logb b (b / a) } 0 := by
  have h₁ : 0 ∈ { y : ℝ | ∃ a b : ℝ, 1 < b ∧ b ≤ a ∧ y = Real.logb a (a / b) + Real.logb b (b / a) } := by

    refine' ⟨2, 2, by norm_num, by norm_num, _⟩

    have h₂ : Real.logb 2 (2 / 2 : ℝ) = 0 := by
      -- logb 2 (2 / 2) = logb 2 1 = 0
      norm_num [Real.logb_eq_iff_rpow_eq]
      <;>
      simp [Real.logb, Real.log_div, Real.log_one, Real.log_pow]
      <;>
      ring_nf
      <;>
      norm_num
      <;>
      linarith [Real.log_pos (by norm_num : (1 : ℝ) < 2)]
    have h₃ : Real.logb 2 (2 / 2 : ℝ) = 0 := by
      -- logb 2 (2 / 2) = logb 2 1 = 0
      norm_num [Real.logb_eq_iff_rpow_eq]
      <;>
      simp [Real.logb, Real.log_div, Real.log_one, Real.log_pow]
      <;>
      ring_nf
      <;>
      norm_num
      <;>
      linarith [Real.log_pos (by norm_num : (1 : ℝ) < 2)]
    -- Sum of the two terms is 0 + 0 = 0
    simp_all [h₂, h₃]
    <;>
    norm_num
    <;>
    linarith
  
  have h₂ : ∀ (y : ℝ), y ∈ { y : ℝ | ∃ a b : ℝ, 1 < b ∧ b ≤ a ∧ y = Real.logb a (a / b) + Real.logb b (b / a) } → y ≤ 0 := by
    intro y hy
    rcases hy with ⟨a, b, hb, hab, rfl⟩
    have h₃ : 1 < b := hb
    have h₄ : b ≤ a := hab
    have h₅ : Real.logb a (a / b) + Real.logb b (b / a) ≤ 0 := by
      have h₅₁ : Real.logb a (a / b) = Real.log (a / b) / Real.log a := by
        rw [Real.logb]
        <;>
        field_simp
      have h₅₂ : Real.logb b (b / a) = Real.log (b / a) / Real.log b := by
        rw [Real.logb]
        <;>
        field_simp
      rw [h₅₁, h₅₂]
      have h₅₃ : Real.log (a / b) = Real.log a - Real.log b := by
        rw [Real.log_div (by linarith) (by linarith)]
        <;>
        ring
      have h₅₄ : Real.log (b / a) = Real.log b - Real.log a := by
        rw [Real.log_div (by linarith) (by linarith)]
        <;>
        ring
      rw [h₅₃, h₅₄]
      have h₅₅ : Real.log a ≥ 0 := Real.log_nonneg (by linarith)
      have h₅₆ : Real.log b > 0 := Real.log_pos (by linarith)
      have h₅₇ : Real.log a - Real.log b ≤ 0 := by
        have h₅₇₁ : Real.log a ≤ Real.log b := Real.log_le_log (by linarith) (by linarith)
        linarith
      have h₅₈ : Real.log b - Real.log a ≤ 0 := by
        have h₅₈₁ : Real.log b ≤ Real.log a := by
          have h₅₈₂ : b ≤ a := by linarith
          exact Real.log_le_log (by linarith) h₅₈₂
        linarith
      have h₅₉ : (Real.log a - Real.log b) / Real.log a + (Real.log b - Real.log a) / Real.log b ≤ 0 := by
        have h₅₉₁ : 0 < Real.log a := by
          have h₅₉₂ : 1 < a := by linarith
          exact Real.log_pos h₅₉₂
        have h₅₉₂ : 0 < Real.log b := by
          have h₅₉₃ : 1 < b := by linarith
          exact Real.log_pos h₅₉₃
        field_simp [h₅₉₁.ne', h₅₉₂.ne']
        rw [div_le_iff (by positivity), ← sub_nonneg]
        nlinarith [mul_nonneg h₅₅ (sub_nonneg.mpr h₅₇), mul_nonneg h₅₅ (sub_nonneg.mpr h₅₈)]
      linarith
    linarith
  
  exact ⟨h₁, h₂⟩
theorem amc12a_2003_p24 :
  IsGreatest { y : ℝ | ∃ a b : ℝ, 1 < b ∧ b ≤ a ∧ y = Real.logb a (a / b) + Real.logb b (b / a) } 0 := by
  have h₀ : 0 ∈ { y : ℝ | ∃ a b : ℝ, 1 < b ∧ b ≤ a ∧ y = Real.logb a (a / b) + Real.logb b (b / a) } := by
    /-
    We need to find \( a \) and \( b \) such that \( 1 < b \), \( b \leq a \), and \( y = \log_a(a/b) + \log_b(b/a) = 0 \). We choose \( a = 2 \) and \( b = 2 \). Then:
    \[ \log_a(a/b) = \log_2(2/2) = \log_2(1) = 0 \]
    \[ \log_b(b/a) = \log_2(2/2) = \log_2(1) = 0 \]
    Thus, \( y = 0 + 0 = 0 \).
    -/
    refine' ⟨2, 2, by norm_num, by norm_num, _⟩
    -- Calculate the logarithms
    have h₁ : Real.logb 2 (2 / 2) = 0 := by
      -- Prove that logb 2 (2 / 2) = 0
      have h₂ : Real.logb 2 (2 / 2) = Real.log (2 / 2) / Real.log 2 := by
        rw [Real.logb]
      rw [h₂]
      have h₃ : Real.log (2 / 2) = 0 := by
        norm_num
      rw [h₃]
      <;> simp [Real.log_one]
      <;> norm_num
    have h₂ : Real.logb 2 (2 / 2) = 0 := by
      -- Prove that logb 2 (2 / 2) = 0
      have h₃ : Real.logb 2 (2 / 2) = Real.log (2 / 2) / Real.log 2 := by
        rw [Real.logb]
      rw [h₃]
      have h₄ : Real.log (2 / 2) = 0 := by
        norm_num
      rw [h₄]
      <;> simp [Real.log_one]
      <;> norm_num
    have h₃ : Real.logb 2 (2 / 2) = 0 := by
      -- Prove that logb 2 (2 / 2) = 0
      have h₄ : Real.logb 2 (2 / 2) = Real.log (2 / 2) / Real.log 2 := by
        rw [Real.logb]
      rw [h₄]
      have h₅ : Real.log (2 / 2) = 0 := by
        norm_num
      rw [h₅]
      <;> simp [Real.log_one]
      <;> norm_num
    -- Sum the logarithms
    simp_all [Real.logb, Real.log_div, Real.log_one, Real.log_mul, Real.log_pow]
    <;> ring_nf at *
    <;> norm_num
    <;> linarith
  
  have h₁ : ∀ y ∈ { y : ℝ | ∃ a b : ℝ, 1 < b ∧ b ≤ a ∧ y = Real.logb a (a / b) + Real.logb b (b / a) }, y ≤ 0 := by
    intro y hy
    rcases hy with ⟨a, b, hb, hab, rfl⟩
    have h₂ : Real.logb a (a / b) + Real.logb b (b / a) ≤ 0 := by
      have h₃ : Real.logb a (a / b) = Real.log (a / b) / Real.log a := by
        rw [Real.logb]
      have h₄ : Real.logb b (b / a) = Real.log (b / a) / Real.log b := by
        rw [Real.logb]
      rw [h₃, h₄]
      have h₅ : Real.log (a / b) = Real.log a - Real.log b := by
        have h₅₁ : a / b > 0 := by
          have h₅₂ : b > 0 := by linarith
          have h₅₃ : a > 0 := by linarith
          positivity
        have h₅₂ : Real.log (a / b) = Real.log a - Real.log b := by
          rw [Real.log_div (by linarith) (by linarith)]
          <;> ring
        exact h₅₂
      have h₆ : Real.log (b / a) = Real.log b - Real.log a := by
        have h₆₁ : b / a > 0 := by
          have h₆₂ : a > 0 := by linarith
          have h₆₃ : b > 0 := by linarith
          positivity
        have h₆₂ : Real.log (b / a) = Real.log b - Real.log a := by
          rw [Real.log_div (by linarith) (by linarith)]
          <;> ring
        exact h₆₂
      rw [h₅, h₆]
      have h₇ : Real.log a > 0 := by
        have h₇₁ : a > 1 := by linarith
        have h₇₂ : Real.log a > 0 := Real.log_pos h₇₁
        exact h₇₂
      have h₈ : Real.log b > 0 := by
        have h₈₁ : b > 1 := by linarith
        have h₈₂ : Real.log b > 0 := Real.log_pos h₈₁
        exact h₈₂
      have h₉ : Real.log a ≠ 0 := by linarith
      have h₁₀ : Real.log b ≠ 0 := by linarith
      field_simp [h₉, h₁₀]
      rw [div_le_iff (by positivity)]
      nlinarith [sq_nonneg (Real.log a - Real.log b), sq_nonneg (Real.log a + Real.log b),
        mul_pos h₇ h₈]
    exact h₂
  
  refine' ⟨h₀, _⟩
  intro y hy
  have h₂ : y ≤ 0 := h₁ y hy
  exact h₂
theorem amc12a_2003_p24 :

    have h₅ : Real.logb (2 : ℝ) (2 / 2 : ℝ) = 0 := by

    <;>
    ring_nf
    <;>
    norm_num
  
  have h₁ : ∀ y ∈ { y : ℝ | ∃ a b : ℝ, 1 < b ∧ b ≤ a ∧ y = Real.logb a (a / b) + Real.logb b (b / a) }, y ≤ 0 := by

        rw [h₁₀]

        rw [h₁₁]

        rw [h₁₂]

    Thus, \( y = 0 \) is in the set.

        rw [h₃]
        have h₄ : Real.log (2 / 2 : ℝ) = 0 := by

      norm_num [h₁, h₂]

      <;>
      ring_nf at *

      norm_num
      <;>

  have h₁ : ∀ y ∈ { y : ℝ | ∃ a b : ℝ, 1 < b ∧ b ≤ a ∧ y = Real.logb a (a / b) + Real.logb b (b / a) }, y ≤ 0 := by

theorem amc12a_2003_p24 :
  IsGreatest { y : ℝ | ∃ a b : ℝ, 1 < b ∧ b ≤ a ∧ y = Real.logb a (a / b) + Real.logb b (b / a) } 0 := by

    have h₄ : Real.logb 2 (2 / 2) = 0 := by
      -- Prove that logb 2 (2 / 2) = 0

      rw [h₅]

    -- Combine the results to get y = 0

    -- Simplify the expression to get y = 0

    <;> linarith

  IsGreatest { y : ℝ | ∃ a b : ℝ, 1 < b ∧ b ≤ a ∧ y = Real.logb a (a / b) + Real.logb b (b / a) } 0 := by
  have h₀ : 0 ∈ { y : ℝ | ∃ a b : ℝ, 1 < b ∧ b ≤ a ∧ y = Real.logb a (a / b) + Real.logb b (b / a) } := by
    /-
    To prove that \( 0 \) is in the set \( \{ y : \mathbb{R} \mid \exists a, b \in \mathbb{R}, 1 < b \land b \leq a \land y = \log_b a + \log_a b \} \), we need to find real numbers \( a \) and \( b \) such that \( 1 < b \leq a \) and \( \log_b a + \log_a b = 0 \).
    
    Consider \( a = b \). Since \( 1 < b \), we have \( b \leq a \) if \( a = b \). Then:
    \[
    \log_b a + \log_a b = \log_b b + \log_b b = 1 + 1 = 2 \neq 0.
    \]
    This does not satisfy the condition. Therefore, we need a different approach.
    
    Consider \( a = 1 \). However, \( a > 1 \) is required, so this is invalid.
    
    Consider \( a = 2 \) and \( b = 1 \). But \( b > 1 \) is required, so this is invalid.
    
    Consider \( a = 2 \) and \( b = 2 \). Then:
    \[
    \log_b a + \log_a b = \log_2 2 + \log_2 2 = 1 + 1 = 2 \neq 0.
    \]
    This does not satisfy the condition.
    
    Consider \( a = 1 \) and \( b = 2 \). But \( a > 1 \) is required, so this is invalid.
    
    Consider \( a = 3 \) and \( b = 2 \). Then:

  IsGreatest { y : ℝ | ∃ a b : ℝ, 1 < b ∧ b ≤ a ∧ y = Real.logb a (a / b) + Real.logb b (b / a) } 0 := by
  have h₁ : 0 ∈ { y : ℝ | ∃ a b : ℝ, 1 < b ∧ b ≤ a ∧ y = Real.logb a (a / b) + Real.logb b (b / a) } := by
    /-
    To prove that 0 is in the set \(\{ y : \mathbb{R} \mid \exists a, b \in \mathbb{R}, 1 < b \land b \leq a \land y = \log_a(a/b) + \log_b(b/a) \}\), we need to find real numbers \(a\) and \(b\) such that \(1 < b\), \(b \leq a\), and \(0 = \log_a(a/b) + \log_b(b/a)\). We choose \(a = 2\) and \(b = 2\), which satisfy \(1 < b\) and \(b \leq a\). Then:
    
    \[
    \log_a(a/b) = \log_2(2/2) = \log_2(1) = 0
    \]
    and
    \[
    \log_b(b/a) = \log_2(2/2) = \log_2(1) = 0.
    \]
    Therefore,
    \[
    \log_a(a/b) + \log_b(b/a) = 0 + 0 = 0.
    \]
    Thus, \(0\) is in the set.
    -/
    use 2, 2
    constructor
    · -- Prove 1 < 2
      norm_num
    constructor
    · -- Prove 2 ≤ 2
      norm_num
    · -- Prove 0 = Real.logb 2 (2 / 2) + Real.logb 2 (2 / 2)
      have h₂ : Real.logb 2 (2 / 2 : ℝ) = 0 := by
        -- Prove Real.logb 2 (2 / 2) = 0
        have h₃ : Real.logb 2 (2 / 2 : ℝ) = Real.log (2 / 2 : ℝ) / Real.log 2 := by
          rw [Real.logb]
          <;> ring_nf
        rw [h₃]
        have h₄ : Real.log (2 / 2 : ℝ) = 0 := by
          -- Prove Real.log (2 / 2) = 0
          have h₅ : (2 : ℝ) / 2 = 1 := by norm_num
          rw [h₅]
          have h₆ : Real.log (1 : ℝ) = 0 := by norm_num
          exact h₆
        rw [h₄]
        <;> norm_num
      have h₃ : Real.logb 2 (2 / 2 : ℝ) = 0 := by
        -- Prove Real.logb 2 (2 / 2) = 0
        have h₄ : Real.logb 2 (2 / 2 : ℝ) = Real.log (2 / 2 : ℝ) / Real.log 2 := by
          rw [Real.logb]
          <;> ring_nf
        rw [h₄]
        have h₅ : Real.log (2 / 2 : ℝ) = 0 := by
          -- Prove Real.log (2 / 2) = 0
          have h₆ : (2 : ℝ) / 2 = 1 := by norm_num
          rw [h₆]
          have h₇ : Real.log (1 : ℝ) = 0 := by norm_num
          exact h₇
        rw [h₅]
        <;> norm_num
      -- Prove 0 = Real.logb 2 (2 / 2) + Real.logb 2 (2 / 2)
      simp_all [h₂, h₃]
      <;> norm_num
      <;> linarith
  
  have h₂ : ∀ y ∈ { y : ℝ | ∃ a b : ℝ, 1 < b ∧ b ≤ a ∧ y = Real.logb a (a / b) + Real.logb b (b / a) }, y ≤ 0 := by
    intro y hy
    rcases hy with ⟨a, b, hb, hab, rfl⟩
    have h₃ : Real.logb a (a / b) + Real.logb b (b / a) ≤ 0 := by
      have h₄ : Real.logb a (a / b) = Real.log (a / b) / Real.log a := by
        rw [Real.logb]
        <;> ring_nf
      have h₅ : Real.logb b (b / a) = Real.log (b / a) / Real.log b := by
        rw [Real.logb]
        <;> ring_nf
      rw [h₄, h₅]
      have h₆ : Real.log (a / b) / Real.log a + Real.log (b / a) / Real.log b ≤ 0 := by
        have h₇ : Real.log (a / b) = Real.log a - Real.log b := by
          rw [Real.log_div (by linarith) (by linarith)]
          <;> ring_nf
        have h₈ : Real.log (b / a) = Real.log b - Real.log a := by
          rw [Real.log_div (by linarith) (by linarith)]
          <;> ring_nf
        rw [h₇, h₈]
        have h₉ : Real.log a > 0 := Real.log_pos (by linarith)
        have h₁₀ : Real.log b > 0 := Real.log_pos (by linarith)
        have h₁₁ : (Real.log a - Real.log b) / Real.log a + (Real.log b - Real.log a) / Real.log b ≤ 0 := by
          have h₁₂ : (Real.log a - Real.log b) / Real.log a + (Real.log b - Real.log a) / Real.log b = ((Real.log a - Real.log b) * Real.log b + (Real.log b - Real.log a) * Real.log a) / (Real.log a * Real.log b) := by
            field_simp [h₉.ne', h₁₀.ne']
            <;> ring_nf
            <;> field_simp [h₉.ne', h₁₀.ne']
            <;> ring_nf
          rw [h₁₂]
          have h₁₃ : (Real.log a - Real.log b) * Real.log b + (Real.log b - Real.log a) * Real.log a = 0 := by
            ring_nf
            <;> nlinarith
          rw [h₁₃]
          have h₁₄ : (0 : ℝ) / (Real.log a * Real.log b) = 0 := by
            field_simp [h₉.ne', h₁₀.ne']
          rw [h₁₄]
          <;> linarith
        exact h₁₁
      exact h₆
    exact h₃
  
  have h₃ : IsGreatest { y : ℝ | ∃ a b : ℝ, 1 < b ∧ b ≤ a ∧ y = Real.logb a (a / b) + Real.logb b (b / a) } 0 := by
    refine' ⟨h₁, _⟩
    intro y hy
    have h₄ : y ≤ 0 := h₂ y hy
    linarith
  
  exact h₃

      Therefore, the equation becomes:
      
      \[
      0 = 0 + 0 = 0
      \]
      
      This satisfies the condition \( 0 = \log_b \left( \frac{a}{b} \right) + \log_a \left( \frac{b}{a} \right) \).

  IsGreatest { y : ℝ | ∃ a b : ℝ, 1 < b ∧ b ≤ a ∧ y = Real.logb a (a / b) + Real.logb b (b / a) } 0 := by
  have h₁ : 0 ∈ { y : ℝ | ∃ a b : ℝ, 1 < b ∧ b ≤ a ∧ y = Real.logb a (a / b) + Real.logb b (b / a) } := by
    /-
    We need to find real numbers \(a\) and \(b\) such that \(1 < b\), \(b \leq a\), and \(y = \log_b a + \log_b b\). We will choose \(a = 2\) and \(b = 1.5\).
    
    First, we verify that \(1 < b\) and \(b \leq a\):
    \[
    1 < 1.5 \quad \text{and} \quad 1.5 \leq 2.
    \]
    Next, we compute \(y\):
    \[
    y = \log_b a + \log_b b = \log_{1.5} 2 + \log_{1.5} 1.5 = \log_{1.5} \left( \frac{2 \cdot 1.5}{1} \right) = \log_{1.5} 3.
    \]
    However, we notice that this is not directly \(0\). Instead, we reconsider the problem and realize that the correct choice is to set \(y = 0\), \(a = 1\), and \(b = 1\). But \(b > 1\) is required, so we need a different approach.
    
    A better choice is to set \(y = 0\), \(a = b\), and \(b = 2\). However, we need to ensure that \(1 < b\) and \(b \leq a\). Here, \(a = b = 2\) satisfies both conditions, but we must verify that \(y = 0\).
    
    Compute \(y\) for \(a = 2\) and \(b = 2\):
    \[
    y = \log_b a + \log_b b = \log_2 2 + \log_2 2 = 1 + 1 = 2.
    \]
    This does not give \(y = 0\).
    
    Another approach is to set \(a = 2\) and \(b = 4\):
    \[
    y = \log_b a + \log_b b = \log_4 2 + \log_4 4 = \frac{1}{2} + 1 = \frac{3}{2}.
    \]
    Still not \(y = 0\).
    
    A more refined choice is \(a = b\). Let \(a = b = 2\):
    \[
    y = \log_b a + \log_b b = \log_2 2 + \log_2 2 = 1 + 1 = 2.
    \]
    This does not give \(y = 0\).
    
    It seems that our initial approach is not working, so we reconsider the problem statement. The problem requires \(y = 0\) for some \(a, b\). We need to find \(a, b\) such that:
    \[
    \log_b a + \log_b b = 0.
    \]
    This can be rewritten as:
    \[
    \log_b a + \log_b b = \log_b (a \cdot b) = 0.
    \]
    Since \(\log_b (a \cdot b) = 0\), it follows that \(a \cdot b = 1\). But \(1 < b\) and \(b \leq a\) imply \(a \geq b > 1\), so \(a \cdot b > 1\), and thus \(\log_b (a \cdot b) > 0\). This is a contradiction.
    
    However, there is a misunderstanding. The expression in the problem is not \(\log_b a + \log_b b\), but rather:
    \[
    y = \log_a \left( \frac{a}{b} \right) + \log_b \left( \frac{b}{a} \right).
    \]
    Let's compute this correctly:
    \[
    y = \log_a \left( \frac{a}{b} \right) + \log_b \left( \frac{b}{a} \right) = \frac{\ln (a / b)}{\ln a} + \frac{\ln (b / a)}{\ln b}.
    \]
    Simplify the expression:
    \[
    y = \frac{\ln a - \ln b}{\ln a} + \frac{\ln b - \ln a}{\ln b} = 1 - \frac{\ln b}{\ln a} + 1 - \frac{\ln a}{\ln b} = 2 - \left( \frac{\ln b}{\ln a} + \frac{\ln a}{\ln b} \right).
    \]
    Let \( t = \frac{\ln b}{\ln a} \). Then:
    \[
    y = 2 - \left( t + \frac{1}{t} \right).
    \]
    Since \( t > 0 \) (because \( \ln b > 0 \) and \( \ln a > 0 \)), we have:
    \[
    t + \frac{1}{t} \geq 2,
    \]
    by the AM-GM inequality, so \( y \leq 0 \). To have \( y = 0 \), we need \( t = 1 \), i.e., \( \ln b = \ln a \) or \( a = b \).
    But \( b \leq a \) and \( 1 < b \), so \( a = b > 1 \).
    However, the problem requires \( 1 < b \leq a \), and if \( a = b \), then:
    \[
    y = \log_a \left( \frac{a}{b} \right) + \log_b \left( \frac{b}{a} \right) = \log_a 1 + \log_b 1 = 0 + 0 = 0.
    \]
    So, we can take \( a = b \). For example, let \( a = b = 2 \):
    \[
    y = \log_2 1 + \log_2 1 = 0 + 0 = 0.
    \]
    This satisfies all conditions: \( 1 < b = 2 \leq a = 2 \) and \( y = 0 \).
    -/
    refine' ⟨2, 2, by norm_num, by norm_num, _⟩
    -- We choose \(a = 2\) and \(b = 2\). 
    -- First, we verify that \(1 < b\) and \(b \leq a\):
    -- \(1 < 2\) and \(2 \leq 2\).
    -- Next, we compute \(y\):
    -- \(y = \log_2 \left( \frac{2}{2} \right) + \log_2 \left( \frac{2}{2} \right) = \log_2 1 + \log_2 1 = 0 + 0 = 0\).
    have h₂ : Real.logb 2 (2 / 2 : ℝ) = Real.logb 2 1 := by norm_num
    have h₃ : Real.logb 2 1 = 0 := by norm_num [Real.logb_eq_zero]
    have h₄ : Real.logb 2 (2 / 2 : ℝ) = 0 := by rw [h₂, h₃]
    have h₅ : Real.logb 2 (2 / 2 : ℝ) + Real.logb 2 (2 / 2 : ℝ) = 0 := by
      rw [h₄]
      <;> norm_num
    norm_num [h₂, h₃, h₄, h₅, Real.logb_eq_zero] at h₂ h₃ h₄ h₅ ⊢
    <;> linarith
  have h₂ : ∀ (y : ℝ), y ∈ { y : ℝ | ∃ a b : ℝ, 1 < b ∧ b ≤ a ∧ y = Real.logb a (a / b) + Real.logb b (b / a) } → y ≤ 0 := by
    intro y hy
    rcases hy with ⟨a, b, hb, hab, rfl⟩
    have h₃ : Real.logb a (a / b) + Real.logb b (b / a) ≤ 0 := by
      have h₄ : 1 < b := by linarith
      have h₅ : b ≤ a := by linarith
      have h₆ : 0 < a := by linarith
      have h₇ : 0 < b := by linarith
      have h₈ : 0

  IsGreatest { y : ℝ | ∃ a b : ℝ, 1 < b ∧ b ≤ a ∧ y = Real.logb a (a / b) + Real.logb b (b / a) } 0 := by

        <;> ring_nf
        <;> norm_num

        linarith

      <;>

      <;>

      <;>
      norm_num at *
      <;>

          have h₉ : 0 < a := by linarith

            · linarith
            · linarith
          have h₁₁ : Real.log (a / b) = Real.log a - Real.log b := by
            rw [Real.log_div (by linarith) (by linarith)]
            <;> ring_nf
          have h₁₂ : Real.log (b / a) = Real.log b - Real.log a := by

            <;> ring_nf

theorem amc12a_2003_p24 :
  IsGreatest { y : ℝ | ∃ a b : ℝ, 1 < b ∧ b ≤ a ∧ y = Real.logb a (a / b) + Real.logb b (b / a) } 0 := by
  have h₁ : 0 ∈ { y : ℝ | ∃ a b : ℝ, 1 < b ∧ b ≤ a ∧ y = Real.logb a (a / b) + Real.logb b (b / a) } := by
    /-
    We need to find real numbers \( a \) and \( b \) such that \( 1 < b \), \( b \leq a \), and the expression \( \text{logb}_a(a / b) + \text{logb}_b(b / a) \) equals \( 0 \).
    
    Recall that:
    \[
    \text{logb}_x(y) = \frac{\ln y}{\ln x}
    \]
    Therefore, the expression becomes:
    \[
    \text{logb}_a(a / b) + \text{logb}_b(b / a) = \frac{\ln(a / b)}{\ln a} + \frac{\ln(b / a)}{\ln b}
    \]
    Simplifying the logarithms:
    \[
    \frac{\ln a - \ln b}{\ln a} + \frac{\ln b - \ln a}{\ln b} = 1 - \frac{\ln b}{\ln a} + \frac{\ln b}{\ln b} - \frac{\ln a}{\ln b} = 1 - \frac{\ln b}{\ln a} + 1 - \frac{\ln a}{\ln b}
    \]
    Combining terms:
    \[
    2 - \frac{\ln b}{\ln a} - \frac{\ln a}{\ln b}
    \]
    However, we can choose \( a = b \) to simplify the problem. If \( a = b \), then:
    \[
    \text{logb}_a(a / a) + \text{logb}_b(b / a) = \text{logb}_a(1) + \text{logb}_b(1) = 0 + 0 = 0
    \]
    This choice of \( a \) and \( b \) satisfies \( 1 < b \leq a \) and \( y = 0 \).
    -/
    use 2, 2
    constructor
    · -- Prove 1 < b
      norm_num
    constructor
    · -- Prove b ≤ a
      norm_num
    · -- Prove y = 0
      have h₂ : Real.logb 2 (2 / 2 : ℝ) + Real.logb 2 (2 / 2 : ℝ) = 0 := by
        have h₃ : Real.logb 2 (2 / 2 : ℝ) = 0 := by
          -- Prove that logb 2 (2 / 2) = 0

          rw [h₄]

            -- Prove that logb 2 1 = 0

          exact h₅
        rw [h₃]
        <;> norm_num
      norm_num at h₂ ⊢
      <;> linarith
  
  have h₂ : ∀ (y : ℝ), y ∈ { y : ℝ | ∃ a b : ℝ, 1 < b ∧ b ≤ a ∧ y = Real.logb a (a / b) + Real.logb b (b / a) } → y ≤ 0 := by
    intro y hy
    rcases hy with ⟨a, b, h₁, h₂, rfl⟩
    have h₃ : Real.logb a (a / b) + Real.logb b (b / a) ≤ 0 := by
      have h₄ : Real.logb a (a / b) = Real.log (a / b) / Real.log a := by
        rw [Real.logb]
      have h₅ : Real.logb b (b / a) = Real.log (b / a) / Real.log b := by

      have h₆ : Real.log (a / b) / Real.log a + Real.log (b / a) / Real.log b ≤ 0 := by

        have h₈ : Real.log (b / a) = Real.log b - Real.log a := by

          <;> ring
        rw [h₇, h₈]
        have h₉ : (Real.log a - Real.log b) / Real.log a + (Real.log b - Real.log a) / Real.log b ≤ 0 := by
          have h₁₀ : Real.log a > 0 := Real.log_pos (by linarith)
          have h₁₁ : Real.log b > 0 := Real.log_pos (by linarith)
          field_simp [h₁₀.ne', h₁₁.ne']
          rw [div_le_iff (by positivity)]
          nlinarith [mul_pos h₁₀ h₁₁, mul_pos h₁₀ h₁₁, mul_pos h₁₀ h₁₁]
        exact h₉
      exact h₆
    exact h₃
  
  have h₃ : IsGreatest { y : ℝ | ∃ a b : ℝ, 1 < b ∧ b ≤ a ∧ y = Real.logb a (a / b) + Real.logb b (b / a) } 0 := by
    refine' ⟨h₁, _⟩
    intro y hy
    have h₄ : y ≤ 0 := h₂ y hy

  exact h₃

theorem amc12a_2003_p24 :
  IsGreatest { y : ℝ | ∃ a b : ℝ, 1 < b ∧ b ≤ a ∧ y = Real.logb a (a / b) + Real.logb b (b / a) } 0 := by
  have h₁ : (0 : ℝ) ∈ { y : ℝ | ∃ a b : ℝ, 1 < b ∧ b ≤ a ∧ y = Real.logb a (a / b) + Real.logb b (b / a) } := by
    /-
    To show that 0 is in the set, we need to find real numbers \( a \) and \( b \) such that \( 1 < b \leq a \) and \( \log_a \left( \frac{a}{b} \right) + \log_b \left( \frac{b}{a} \right) = 0 \). 
    
    We can choose \( a = 2 \) and \( b = 2 \). Then, we have \( 1 < 2 \leq 2 \) and:
    
    \[
    \log_2 \left( \frac{2}{2} \right) + \log_2 \left( \frac{2}{2} \right) = \log_2 1 + \log_2 1 = 0 + 0 = 0.
    \]
    
    Therefore, \( 0 \) is indeed in the set.
    -/
    refine' ⟨2, 2, by norm_num, by norm_num, _⟩
    have h₂ : Real.logb 2 (2 / 2) = 0 := by
      -- Prove that logb 2 (2 / 2) = 0
      have h₃ : (2 : ℝ) / 2 = 1 := by norm_num
      rw [h₃]
      -- Simplify logb 2 1 to 0
      have h₄ : Real.logb 2 (1 : ℝ) = 0 := by
        rw [Real.logb]
        -- Use the property of logarithms to show that log 1 = 0
        have h₅ : Real.log (1 : ℝ) = 0 := by norm_num
        have h₆ : Real.log (2 : ℝ) > 0 := Real.log_pos (by norm_num)
        field_simp [h₅, h₆.ne']
        <;> ring_nf
        <;> norm_num
      rw [h₄]
    have h₃ : Real.logb 2 (2 / 2) = 0 := by
      -- Prove that logb 2 (2 / 2) = 0
      have h₄ : (2 : ℝ) / 2 = 1 := by norm_num
      rw [h₄]
      -- Simplify logb 2 1 to 0
      have h₅ : Real.logb 2 (1 : ℝ) = 0 := by
        rw [Real.logb]
        -- Use the property of logarithms to show that log 1 = 0
        have h₆ : Real.log (1 : ℝ) = 0 := by norm_num
        have h₇ : Real.log (2 : ℝ) > 0 := Real.log_pos (by norm_num)
        field_simp [h₆, h₇.ne']
        <;> ring_nf
        <;> norm_num
      rw [h₅]
    -- Combine the results to show that the sum is 0
    simp_all [Real.logb, Real.log_div, Real.log_mul, Real.log_rpow, Real.log_pow]
    <;> field_simp [Real.log_mul, Real.log_div, Real.log_rpow, Real.log_pow] at *
    <;> ring_nf at *
    <;> norm_num at *
    <;> linarith
  have h₂ : ∀ y ∈ { y : ℝ | ∃ a b : ℝ, 1 < b ∧ b ≤ a ∧ y = Real.logb a (a / b) + Real.logb b (b / a) }, y ≤ 0 := by
    intro y hy

      rw [h₆, h₇]

        rw [Real.log_div (by linarith) (by linarith)]
      have h₉ : Real.log (b / a) = Real.log b - Real.log a := by
        rw [Real.log_div (by linarith) (by linarith)]

          linarith
        have h₁₄ : (Real.log a - Real.log b) / Real.log a + ((Real.log b - Real.log a) / Real.log b) ≤ 0 := by

          have h₁₆ : 0 < Real.log b := by linarith

  IsGreatest { y : ℝ | ∃ a b : ℝ, 1 < b ∧ b ≤ a ∧ y = Real.logb a (a / b) + Real.logb b (b / a) } 0 := by
  have h₀ : 0 ∈ { y : ℝ | ∃ a b : ℝ, 1 < b ∧ b ≤ a ∧ y = Real.logb a (a / b) + Real.logb b (b / a) } := by
    /-
    To prove that \( 0 \) is in the set, we need to find real numbers \( a \) and \( b \) such that \( 1 < b \), \( b \leq a \), and \( 0 = \log_a \left( \frac{a}{b} \right) + \log_b \left( \frac{b}{a} \right) \).
    
    First, let's simplify the expression:
    \[
    \log_a \left( \frac{a}{b} \right) + \log_b \left( \frac{b}{a} \right)
    \]
    Using the logarithm power rule \( \log_b x = \frac{\ln x}{\ln b} \) and the change of base formula \( \log_b a = \frac{\ln a}{\ln b} \), we can rewrite each term:
    \[
    \log_a \left( \frac{a}{b} \right) = \frac{\ln \left( \frac{a}{b} \right)}{\ln a} = \frac{\ln a - \ln b}{\ln a}
    \]
    \[
    \log_b \left( \frac{b}{a} \right) = \frac{\ln \left( \frac{b}{a} \right)}{\ln b} = \frac{\ln b - \ln a}{\ln b}
    \]
    Adding these two expressions together:
    \[
    \frac{\ln a - \ln b}{\ln a} + \frac{\ln b - \ln a}{\ln b} = \frac{\ln a - \ln b}{\ln a} - \frac{\ln a - \ln b}{\ln b}
    \]
    \[
    = (\ln a - \ln b) \left( \frac{1}{\ln a} - \frac{1}{\ln b} \right)
    \]
    \[
    = (\ln a - \ln b) \left( \frac{\ln b - \ln a}{\ln a \ln b} \right)
    \]
    \[
    = \frac{(\ln a - \ln b)(\ln b - \ln a)}{\ln a \ln b}
    \]
    \[
    = \frac{-(\ln a - \ln b)^2}{\ln a \ln b}
    \]
    \[
    = -\frac{(\ln a - \ln b)^2}{\ln a \ln b}
    \]
    However, this seems incorrect because \( (\ln a - \ln b)^2 \geq 0 \) and \( \ln a \ln b > 0 \), so the expression would be non-positive. But we need the expression to be \( 0 \).
    
    Instead, let's choose \( a = b \), which trivially satisfies \( b \leq a \), and check if the expression becomes \( 0 \). But if \( a = b \), then \( \frac{a}{b} = 1 \) and \( \frac{b}{a} = 1 \), so:
    \[
    \log_a \left( \frac{a}{b} \right) = \log_b \left( \frac{b}{a} \right) = 0
    \]

    refine' ⟨h₀, _⟩

  IsGreatest { y : ℝ | ∃ a b : ℝ, 1 < b ∧ b ≤ a ∧ y = Real.logb a (a / b) + Real.logb b (b / a) } 0 := by
  have h₀ : 0 ∈ { y : ℝ | ∃ a b : ℝ, 1 < b ∧ b ≤ a ∧ y = Real.logb a (a / b) + Real.logb b (b / a) } := by
    use 2, 2
    constructor
    · norm_num
    constructor
    · norm_num
    · norm_num [Real.logb_eq_zero]
      <;>
      simp [Real.logb, Real.log_div, Real.log_mul, Real.log_rpow, Real.log_pow]
      <;>
      ring_nf
      <;>
      field_simp [Real.log_mul, Real.log_rpow, Real.log_pow]
      <;>
      ring_nf
      <;>
      norm_num
      <;>
      linarith [Real.log_pos (by norm_num : (1 : ℝ) < 2)]
  
  have h₁ : ∀ (y : ℝ), y ∈ { y : ℝ | ∃ a b : ℝ, 1 < b ∧ b ≤ a ∧ y = Real.logb a (a / b) + Real.logb b (b / a) } → y ≤ 0 := by
    intro y hy
    rcases hy with ⟨a, b, hb, hab, rfl⟩
    have h₁ : Real.logb a (a / b) + Real.logb b (b / a) ≤ 0 := by
      have h₂ : Real.logb a (a / b) = Real.log (a / b) / Real.log a := by
        rw [Real.logb]
        <;>
        simp [Real.log_div, Real.log_mul, Real.log_rpow, Real.log_pow]
        <;>
        ring_nf
        <;>
        field_simp [Real.log_mul, Real.log_rpow, Real.log_pow]
        <;>
        ring_nf
        <;>
        norm_num
        <;>
        linarith [Real.log_pos (by norm_num : (1 : ℝ) < 2)]
      have h₃ : Real.logb b (b / a) = Real.log (b / a) / Real.log b := by
        rw [Real.logb]
        <;>
        simp [Real.log_div, Real.log_mul, Real.log_rpow, Real.log_pow]
        <;>
        ring_nf
        <;>
        field_simp [Real.log_mul, Real.log_rpow, Real.log_pow]
        <;>
        ring_nf
        <;>
        norm_num
        <;>
        linarith [Real.log_pos (by norm_num : (1 : ℝ) < 2)]
      rw [h₂, h₃]
      have h₄ : Real.log (a / b) = Real.log a - Real.log b := by
        rw [Real.log_div (by linarith) (by linarith)]
      have h₅ : Real.log (b / a) = Real.log b - Real.log a := by
        rw [Real.log_div (by linarith) (by linarith)]
      rw [h₄, h₅]
      have h₆ : (Real.log a - Real.log b) / Real.log a + (Real.log b - Real.log a) / Real.log b ≤ 0 := by
        have h₇ : Real.log a > 0 := Real.log_pos (by linarith)
        have h₈ : Real.log b > 0 := Real.log_pos (by linarith)
        have h₉ : Real.log a ≠ 0 := by linarith
        have h₁₀ : Real.log b ≠ 0 := by linarith
        field_simp [h₉, h₁₀]
        rw [div_le_iff (by positivity), ← sub_nonneg]
        ring_nf
        nlinarith [sq_nonneg (Real.log a - Real.log b), Real.log_le_log (by linarith) (by linarith : a ≥ b),
          mul_pos h₇ h₈]
      linarith
    exact h₁
  
  have h₂ : IsGreatest { y : ℝ | ∃ a b : ℝ, 1 < b ∧ b ≤ a ∧ y = Real.logb a (a / b) + Real.logb b (b / a) } 0 := by
    refine' ⟨h₀, _⟩
    intro y hy
    exact h₁ y hy
  
  exact h₂
theorem amc12a_2003_p24 :
  IsGreatest { y : ℝ | ∃ a b : ℝ, 1 < b ∧ b ≤ a ∧ y = Real.logb a (a / b) + Real.logb b (b / a) } 0 := by
  have h₀ : 0 ∈ { y : ℝ | ∃ a b : ℝ, 1 < b ∧ b ≤ a ∧ y = Real.logb a (a / b) + Real.logb b (b / a) } := by
    refine' ⟨2, 1.5, by norm_num, by norm_num, _⟩
    -- We choose a = 2 and b = 1.5
    have h₁ : Real.logb 2 (2 / 1.5 : ℝ) + Real.logb (1.5 : ℝ) ((1.5 : ℝ) / 2) = 0 := by
      -- Calculate the logarithmic expressions
      have h₂ : Real.logb 2 (2 / 1.5 : ℝ) = Real.log (2 / 1.5) / Real.log 2 := by
        rw [Real.logb]
        <;> norm_num
      have h₃ : Real.logb (1.5 : ℝ) ((1.5 : ℝ) / 2) = Real.log ((1.5 : ℝ) / 2) / Real.log (1.5 : ℝ) := by
        rw [Real.logb]
        <;> norm_num
      rw [h₂, h₃]
      have h₄ : Real.log (2 / 1.5) / Real.log 2 + (Real.log ((1.5 : ℝ) / 2) / Real.log (1.5 : ℝ)) = 0 := by
        -- Use the property of logarithms to simplify
        have h₅ : Real.log (2 / 1.5) = Real.log 2 - Real.log (1.5 : ℝ) := by
          rw [Real.log_div (by norm_num) (by norm_num)]
          <;> ring_nf
          <;> norm_num
        have h₆ : Real.log ((1.5 : ℝ) / 2) = Real.log (1.5 : ℝ) - Real.log 2 := by
          rw [Real.log_div (by norm_num) (by norm_num)]
          <;> ring_nf
          <;> norm_num
        rw [h₅, h₆]
        have h₇ : Real.log 2 ≠ 0 := by
          norm_num [Real.log_pos]
          <;> linarith [Real.log_pos (by norm_num : (1 : ℝ) < 2)]
        have h₈ : Real.log (1.5 : ℝ) ≠ 0 := by
          have h₉ : Real.log (1.5 : ℝ) > 0 := Real.log_pos (by norm_num)
          linarith
        field_simp [h₇, h₈]
        <;> ring_nf
        <;> field_simp [h₇, h₈]
        <;> ring_nf
        <;> linarith [Real.log_pos (by norm_num : (1 : ℝ) < 2), Real.log_pos (by norm_num : (1 : ℝ) < 1.5)]
      linarith
    -- Simplify the final expression
    simpa using h₁
  
  have h₁ : IsGreatest { y : ℝ | ∃ a b : ℝ, 1 < b ∧ b ≤ a ∧ y = Real.logb a (a / b) + Real.logb b (b / a) } 0 := by
    refine' ⟨h₀, _⟩
    -- We need to show that 0 is the greatest element in the set.
    rintro y ⟨a, b, h₁, h₂, h₃⟩
    have h₄ : y = Real.logb a (a / b) + Real.logb b (b / a) := by rw [h₃]
    have h₅ : 0 ≤ Real.logb a (a / b) + Real.logb b (b / a) := by
      -- We need to show that the sum of the logarithms is non-negative.
      by_cases h₅ : a = b
      · -- If a = b, the sum is 0.
        rw [h₅]
        simp [Real.logb, Real.log_div, Real.log_mul, Real.log_rpow, Real.log_pow, h₁, h₂, le_of_lt]
        <;> field_simp [Real.log_div, Real.log_mul, Real.log_rpow, Real.log_pow, h₁, h₂, le_of_lt]
        <;> ring_nf
        <;> simp_all [Real.log_div, Real.log_mul, Real.log_rpow, Real.log_pow, h₁, h₂, le_of_lt]
        <;> linarith
      · -- If a ≠ b, we need to show that the sum is non-negative.
        have h₆ : a ≠ b := h₅
        have h₇ : 1 < b := h₁
        have h₈ : b ≤ a := h₂
        have h₉ : 0 < a := by
          by_contra h
          have h₁₀ : a ≤ 0 := by linarith
          have h₁₁ : b > 1 := h₁
          have h₁₂ : b ≤ a := h₂
          linarith
        have h₁₀ : 0 < b := by linarith
        have h₁₁ : 0 < a / b := by positivity
        have h₁₂ : 0 < b / a := by positivity
        have h₁₃ : Real.logb a (a / b) + Real.logb b (b / a) ≥ 0 := by
          -- Use the properties of logarithms to show the sum is non-negative.
          have h₁₄ : Real.logb a (a / b) = Real.log (a / b) / Real.log a := by
            rw [Real.logb]
            <;> field_simp [Real.log_div, Real.log_mul, Real.log_rpow, Real.log_pow, h₁, h₂, le_of_lt]
          have h₁₅ : Real.logb b (b / a) = Real.log (b / a) / Real.log b := by
            rw [Real.logb]
            <;> field_simp [Real.log_div, Real.log_mul, Real.log_rpow, Real.log_pow, h₁, h₂, le_of_lt]
          rw [h₁₄, h₁₅]
          have h₁₆ : Real.log (a / b) / Real.log a + Real.log (b / a) / Real.log b ≥ 0 := by
            -- Use the fact that the logarithms of the reciprocals cancel out.
            have h₁₇ : Real.log (a / b) = Real.log a - Real.log b := by
              rw [Real.log_div (by positivity) (by positivity)]
            have h₁₈ : Real.log (b / a) = Real.log b - Real.log a := by
              rw [Real.log_div (by positivity) (by positivity)]
            rw [h₁₇, h₁₈]
            have h₁₉ : (Real.log a - Real.log b) / Real.log a + (Real.log b - Real.log a) / Real.log b ≥ 0 := by
              have h₂₀ : Real.log a ≠ 0 := by
                -- Prove that the logarithm of a positive number is not zero.
                have h₂₁ : a > 1 := by
                  by_contra h₂₁
                  have h₂₂ : a ≤ 1 := by linarith
                  have h₂₃ : b > 1 := h₁
                  have h₂₄ : b ≤ a := h₂
                  linarith
                have h₂₂ : Real.log a > 0 := Real.log_pos (by linarith)
                linarith
              have h₂₁ : Real.log b ≠ 0 := by
                -- Prove that the logarithm of a positive number is not zero.
                have h₂₂ : b > 1 := h₁
                have h₂₃ : Real.log b > 0 := Real.log_pos (by linarith)
                linarith
              field_simp [h₂₀, h₂₁]
              rw [le_div_iff (by positivity)]
              nlinarith [Real.log_le_sub_one_of_pos (by positivity : 0 < (a : ℝ)), Real.log_le_sub_one_of_pos (by positivity : 0 < (b : ℝ))]
            linarith
          linarith
        linarith
    -- We have shown that y ≤ 0.
    have h₆ : y ≤ 0 := by
      have h₇ : y = Real.logb a (a / b) + Real.logb b (b / a) := by rw [h₄]
      rw [h₇]
      linarith
    linarith
  
  exact h₁
theorem amc12a_2003_p24 :
  IsGreatest { y : ℝ | ∃ a b : ℝ, 1 < b ∧ b ≤ a ∧ y = Real.logb a (a / b) + Real.logb b (b / a) } 0 := by
  constructor
  · -- Prove that 0 is a member of the set
    use 2, 2
    constructor
    · -- Prove 1 < b
      norm_num
    · -- Prove b ≤ a and y = Real.logb a (a / b) + Real.logb b (b / a)
      constructor
      · -- Prove b ≤ a
        norm_num
      · -- Prove y = Real.logb a (a / b) + Real.logb b (b / a)
        have h₁ : Real.logb (2 : ℝ) (2 / 2) = Real.logb (2 : ℝ) 1 := by norm_num
        have h₂ : Real.logb (2 : ℝ) 1 = 0 := by
          rw [Real.logb]
          field_simp [Real.log_ne_zero_of_pos_of_ne_one (by norm_num : (0 : ℝ) < 2) (by norm_num : (2 : ℝ) ≠ 1)]
          <;> ring_nf
          <;> simp [Real.log_one]
        have h₃ : Real.logb (2 : ℝ) (2 / 2) = 0 := by rw [h₁, h₂]
        have h₄ : Real.logb (2 : ℝ) (2 / 2) + Real.logb (2 : ℝ) (2 / 2) = 0 := by
          rw [h₃]
          <;> norm_num
        simp_all [Real.logb, Real.log_div, Real.log_mul, Real.log_pow]
        <;> field_simp [Real.log_ne_zero_of_pos_of_ne_one]
        <;> ring_nf
        <;> norm_num
        <;> linarith
  · -- Prove that 0 is the greatest element in the set
    intro y hy
    rcases hy with ⟨a, b, h₁, h₂, h₃⟩
    have h₄ : y = Real.logb a (a / b) + Real.logb b (b / a) := by rw [h₃]
    rw [h₄]
    have h₅ : Real.logb a (a / b) = Real.log (a / b) / Real.log a := by
      rw [Real.logb]
    have h₆ : Real.logb b (b / a) = Real.log (b / a) / Real.log b := by
      rw [Real.logb]
    rw [h₅, h₆]
    have h₇ : Real.log (a / b) = Real.log a - Real.log b := by
      rw [Real.log_div (by
        -- Prove that a > 0
        linarith) (by
        -- Prove that b > 0
        linarith)]
    have h₈ : Real.log (b / a) = Real.log b - Real.log a := by
      rw [Real.log_div (by
        -- Prove that b > 0
        linarith) (by
        -- Prove that a > 0
        linarith)]
    rw [h₇, h₈]
    have h₉ : (Real.log a - Real.log b) / Real.log a + (Real.log b - Real.log a) / Real.log b = 0 := by
      have h₁₀ : Real.log a > 0 := Real.log_pos (by linarith)
      have h₁₁ : Real.log b > 0 := Real.log_pos (by linarith)
      field_simp [h₁₀.ne', h₁₁.ne']
      <;> ring_nf
      <;> field_simp [h₁₀.ne', h₁₁.ne']
      <;> nlinarith [Real.log_pos (by linarith : (1 : ℝ) < b)]
    linarith
theorem amc12a_2003_p24 :
  IsGreatest { y : ℝ | ∃ a b : ℝ, 1 < b ∧ b ≤ a ∧ y = Real.logb a (a / b) + Real.logb b (b / a) } 0 := by
  refine' ⟨fun y hy => by simp_all [Set.mem_setOf_eq, IsGreatest], _⟩
  -- We need to prove that 0 is the greatest element in the set.
  refine' ⟨_, _⟩
  · -- Prove that 0 is in the set.
    use 2, 2
    constructor
    · -- Prove that 1 < 2.
      norm_num
    · constructor
      · -- Prove that 2 ≤ 2.
        norm_num
      · -- Prove that 0 = logb 2 (2 / 2) + logb 2 (2 / 2).
        have h₁ : Real.logb (2 : ℝ) (2 / 2 : ℝ) = Real.logb (2 : ℝ) (1 : ℝ) := by norm_num
        have h₂ : Real.logb (2 : ℝ) (1 : ℝ) = 0 := by
          simp [Real.logb, Real.log_one]
          <;> norm_num
        have h₃ : Real.logb (2 : ℝ) (2 / 2 : ℝ) = 0 := by rw [h₁, h₂]
        have h₄ : Real.logb (2 : ℝ) (2 / 2 : ℝ) + Real.logb (2 : ℝ) (2 / 2 : ℝ) = 0 := by linarith
        norm_num [Real.logb, Real.log_one] at *
        <;> linarith
  · -- Prove that for any y in the set, y ≤ 0.
    intro y hy
    rcases hy with ⟨a, b, h₁, h₂, rfl⟩
    have h₃ : Real.logb a (a / b) + Real.logb b (b / a) ≤ 0 := by
      have h₄ : Real.logb a (a / b) = Real.logb a a - Real.logb a b := by
        rw [Real.logb_div (by linarith) (by linarith)]
        <;> ring_nf
      have h₅ : Real.logb b (b / a) = Real.logb b b - Real.logb b a := by
        rw [Real.logb_div (by linarith) (by linarith)]
        <;> ring_nf
      rw [h₄, h₅]
      have h₆ : Real.logb a a = 1 := by
        rw [Real.logb]
        field_simp [Real.log_ne_zero_of_pos_of_ne_one (by linarith : (0 : ℝ) < a) (by
          intro h
          linarith)]
        <;> ring_nf
        <;> simp_all [Real.log_one]
        <;> linarith
      have h₇ : Real.logb b b = 1 := by
        rw [Real.logb]
        field_simp [Real.log_ne_zero_of_pos_of_ne_one (by linarith : (0 : ℝ) < b) (by
          intro h
          linarith)]
        <;> ring_nf
        <;> simp_all [Real.log_one]
        <;> linarith
      rw [h₆, h₇]
      have h₈ : Real.logb a b = Real.logb b a := by
        have h₉ : Real.logb a b = Real.logb b a := by
          rw [Real.logb, Real.logb]
          field_simp [Real.log_div, Real.log_mul, Real.log_rpow, Real.log_inv, Real.log_pow]
          <;> ring_nf
          <;> field_simp [Real.log_mul, Real.log_rpow, Real.log_inv, Real.log_pow]
          <;> ring_nf
          <;> field_simp [Real.log_mul, Real.log_rpow, Real.log_inv, Real.log_pow]
          <;> linarith
        rw [h₉]
      rw [h₈]
      linarith
    exact h₃
theorem amc12a_2003_p24 :
  IsGreatest { y : ℝ | ∃ a b : ℝ, 1 < b ∧ b ≤ a ∧ y = Real.logb a (a / b) + Real.logb b (b / a) } 0 := by
  have h₀ : (0 : ℝ) ∈ { y : ℝ | ∃ a b : ℝ, 1 < b ∧ b ≤ a ∧ y = Real.logb a (a / b) + Real.logb b (b / a) } := by
    refine' ⟨2, 2, by norm_num, by norm_num, _⟩
    -- We choose a = 2 and b = 2.
    -- First, we need to compute Real.logb 2 (2 / 2) + Real.logb 2 (2 / 2).
    have h₁ : Real.logb 2 (2 / 2) = 0 := by
      -- Real.logb 2 (2 / 2) = Real.logb 2 1 = 0 because Real.log 1 = 0.
      have h₂ : Real.logb 2 (2 / 2) = Real.logb 2 1 := by norm_num
      rw [h₂]
      -- Real.logb 2 1 = Real.log 1 / Real.log 2 = 0 / Real.log 2 = 0.
      have h₃ : Real.logb 2 1 = 0 := by
        simp [Real.logb, Real.log_one]
        <;> ring
      rw [h₃]
    have h₂ : Real.logb 2 (2 / 2) = 0 := by
      -- Real.logb 2 (2 / 2) = Real.logb 2 1 = 0 because Real.log 1 = 0.
      have h₃ : Real.logb 2 (2 / 2) = Real.logb 2 1 := by norm_num
      rw [h₃]
      -- Real.logb 2 1 = Real.log 1 / Real.log 2 = 0 / Real.log 2 = 0.
      have h₄ : Real.logb 2 1 = 0 := by
        simp [Real.logb, Real.log_one]
        <;> ring
      rw [h₄]
    have h₃ : Real.logb 2 (2 / 2) = 0 := by
      -- Real.logb 2 (2 / 2) = Real.logb 2 1 = 0 because Real.log 1 = 0.
      have h₄ : Real.logb 2 (2 / 2) = Real.logb 2 1 := by norm_num
      rw [h₄]
      -- Real.logb 2 1 = Real.log 1 / Real.log 2 = 0 / Real.log 2 = 0.
      have h₅ : Real.logb 2 1 = 0 := by
        simp [Real.logb, Real.log_one]
        <;> ring
      rw [h₅]
    -- Now we need to compute Real.logb 2 (2 / 2) + Real.logb 2 (2 / 2).
    norm_num [h₁, h₂, h₃]
    <;>
    simp_all [Real.logb, Real.log_div, Real.log_one, Real.log_mul, Real.log_rpow, Real.log_pow]
    <;>
    ring_nf
    <;>
    norm_num
    <;>
    linarith
  have h₁ : IsGreatest { y : ℝ | ∃ a b : ℝ, 1 < b ∧ b ≤ a ∧ y = Real.logb a (a / b) + Real.logb b (b / a) } 0 := by
    refine' ⟨h₀, _⟩
    rintro y ⟨a, b, h₂, h₃, h₄⟩
    -- We need to show that y ≤ 0 for any y in the set.
    have h₅ : y = Real.logb a (a / b) + Real.logb b (b / a) := by rw [h₄]
    rw [h₅]
    -- We need to show that Real.logb a (a / b) + Real.logb b (b / a) ≤ 0.
    have h₆ : Real.logb a (a / b) + Real.logb b (b / a) ≤ 0 := by
      have h₇ : Real.logb a (a / b) = Real.log (a / b) / Real.log a := by
        rw [Real.logb]
      have h₈ : Real.logb b (b / a) = Real.log (b / a) / Real.log b := by
        rw [Real.logb]
      rw [h₇, h₈]
      have h₉ : Real.log (a / b) = Real.log a - Real.log b := by
        rw [Real.log_div (by linarith) (by linarith)]
      have h₁₀ : Real.log (b / a) = Real.log b - Real.log a := by
        rw [Real.log_div (by linarith) (by linarith)]
      rw [h₉, h₁₀]
      have h₁₁ : (Real.log a - Real.log b) / Real.log a + ((Real.log b - Real.log a) / Real.log b) ≤ 0 := by
        have h₁₂ : Real.log a ≠ 0 := by
          have h₁₃ : 1 < (b : ℝ) := by assumption_mod_cast
          have h₁₄ : 1 < (a : ℝ) := by linarith
          have h₁₅ : Real.log a > 0 := Real.log_pos (by linarith)
          linarith
        have h₁₃ : Real.log b ≠ 0 := by
          have h₁₄ : 1 < (b : ℝ) := by assumption_mod_cast
          have h₁₅ : Real.log b > 0 := Real.log_pos (by linarith)
          linarith
        field_simp [h₁₂, h₁₃]
        rw [div_le_iff (by
          have h₁₄ : 0 < Real.log a := by
            have h₁₅ : 1 < (a : ℝ) := by linarith
            have h₁₆ : Real.log a > 0 := Real.log_pos (by linarith)
            linarith
          have h₁₅ : 0 < Real.log b := by
            have h₁₆ : 1 < (b : ℝ) := by assumption_mod_cast
            have h₁₇ : Real.log b > 0 := Real.log_pos (by linarith)
            linarith
          positivity)]
        nlinarith [sq_nonneg (Real.log a - Real.log b), Real.log_pos (by linarith : (1 : ℝ) < b)]
      linarith
    linarith
  exact h₁
theorem amc12a_2003_p24 :
  IsGreatest { y : ℝ | ∃ a b : ℝ, 1 < b ∧ b ≤ a ∧ y = Real.logb a (a / b) + Real.logb b (b / a) } 0 := by
  have h₀ : (0 : ℝ) ∈ { y : ℝ | ∃ a b : ℝ, 1 < b ∧ b ≤ a ∧ y = Real.logb a (a / b) + Real.logb b (b / a) } := by
    /-
    We need to find \( a \) and \( b \) such that \( 1 < b \), \( b \leq a \), and \( 0 = \log_b \left( \frac{a}{b} \right) + \log_b \left( \frac{b}{a} \right) \).
    
    We can choose \( a = b = 2 \):
    - \( 1 < 2 \) is true.
    - \( 2 \leq 2 \) is true.
    - Now, we compute:
      \[

    rw [h₅]
    have h₆ : Real.logb a (a / b) = Real.log (a / b) / Real.log a := by
      rw [Real.logb]
    have h₇ : Real.logb b (b / a) = Real.log (b / a) / Real.log b := by
      rw [Real.logb]
    rw [h₆, h₇]

      have h₉ : a > 0 := by
        by_contra h₉
        have h₁₀ : a ≤ 0 := by linarith
        have h₁₁ : b ≤ a := h₃
        have h₁₂ : b ≤ 0 := by linarith
        have h₁₃ : b > 1 := h₂

        by_contra h₁₀
        have h₁₁ : b ≤ 0 := by linarith
        have h₁₂ : b > 1 := h₂
        linarith
      have h₁₁ : a / b > 0 := by
        have h₁₂ : a > 0 := by linarith
        have h₁₃ : b > 0 := by linarith

        rw [Real.log_div (by positivity) (by positivity)]
        <;> ring
      rw [h₁₂]
      <;> ring
    have h₉ : Real.log (b / a) = Real.log b - Real.log a := by
      have h₁₀ : a > 0 := by
        by_contra h₁₀
        have h₁₁ : a ≤ 0 := by linarith
        have h₁₂ : b ≤ a := h₃
        have h₁₃ : b ≤ 0 := by linarith
        have h₁₄ : b > 1 := h₂
        linarith
      have h₁₁ : b > 0 := by
        by_contra h₁₁
        have h₁₂ : b ≤ 0 := by linarith
        have h₁₃ : b > 1 := h₂
        linarith
      have h₁₂ : b / a > 0 := by
        have h₁₃ : a > 0 := by linarith
        have h₁₄ : b > 0 := by linarith
        positivity
      have h₁₃ : Real.log (b / a) = Real.log b - Real.log a := by
        rw [Real.log_div (by positivity) (by positivity)]
        <;> ring
      rw [h₁₃]
      <;> ring

      have h₁₁ : a ≥ b := by linarith

        -- Prove that a > 0

          by_contra h₁₃

          have h₁₆ : b ≤ 0 := by linarith

    have h₁₁ : Real.log a ≠ 0 := by
      have h₁₂ : a > 1 := by
        by_contra h₁₂
        have h₁₃ : a ≤ 1 := by linarith
        have h₁₄ : a > 0 := by
          by_contra h₁₄

          have h₁₇ : b ≤ 0 := by linarith
          have h₁₈ : b > 1 := h₂
          linarith
        have h₁₅ : Real.log a ≤ 0 := Real.log_nonpos (by linarith) (by linarith)
        have h₁₆ : b ≤ a := h₃
        have h₁₇ : b > 1 := h₂
        have h₁₈ : Real.log b > 0 := Real.log_pos (by linarith)
        have h₁₉ : Real.log a ≥ Real.log b := h₁₀
        linarith
      have h₁₃ : Real.log a > 0 := Real.log_pos (by linarith)
      linarith
   
theorem amc12a_2003_p24 :
  IsGreatest { y : ℝ | ∃ a b : ℝ, 1 < b ∧ b ≤ a ∧ y = Real.logb a (a / b) + Real.logb b (b / a) } 0 := by
  have h₁ : (0 : ℝ) ∈ { y : ℝ | ∃ a b : ℝ, 1 < b ∧ b ≤ a ∧ y = Real.logb a (a / b) + Real.logb b (b / a) } := by

    To prove that 0 is in the set \( \{ y : \mathbb{R} \mid \exists a, b \in \mathbb{R}, 1 < b \land b \leq a \land y = \log_b \left( \frac{a}{b} \right) + \log_a \left( \frac{b}{a} \right) \} \), we need to find real numbers \( a \) and \( b \) such that \( 1 < b \), \( b \leq a \), and the expression for \( y \) simplifies to 0.
    
    We choose \( a = b = 2 \), which satisfies \( 1 < b \leq a \).
    
    First, compute \( \log_b \left( \frac{a}{b} \right) \):
    \[
    \log_b \left( \frac{a}{b} \right) = \log_2 \left( \frac{2}{2} \right) = \log_2 (1) = 0.
    \]
    
    Next, compute \( \log_a \left( \frac{b}{a} \right) \):
    \[
    \log_a \left( \frac{b}{a} \right) = \log_2 \left( \frac{2}{2} \right) = \log_2 (1) = 0.
    \]
    
    Therefore, the sum of these logarithms is:
    \[
    y = 0 + 0 = 0.
    \]
    Thus, \( y = 0 \) is in the set.
    -/
    use 2, 2
    constructor
    · -- Prove that 1 < 2
      norm_num
    · constructor
      · -- Prove that 2 ≤ 2
        norm_num
      · -- Prove that y = 0
        have h₂ : Real.logb 2 (2 / 2 : ℝ) = 0 := by
          -- Prove that log_2(2/2) = 0

          <;> ring_nf
          <;> field_simp
          <;> ring_nf
          <;> norm_num
        have h₄ : Real.logb 2 (2 / 2 : ℝ) + Real.logb 2 (2 / 2 : ℝ) = 0 := by
          rw [h₂]
          <;> norm_num
        -- Simplify the expression to show y = 0
        norm_num [Real.logb, Real.log_div, Real.log_mul, Real.log_rpow] at h₂ h₄ ⊢ <;>
          simp_all [h₂, h₄]
        <;>
          norm_num
        <;>
          ring_nf at *
        <;>
          field_simp at *
        <;>
          linarith

    \]
    \[

    \]

    \[

    \]

  IsGreatest { y : ℝ | ∃ a b : ℝ, 1 < b ∧ b ≤ a ∧ y = Real.logb a (a / b) + Real.logb b (b / a) } 0 := by

      have h₁ : (0 : ℝ) ∈ { y : ℝ | ∃ a b : ℝ, 1 < b ∧ b ≤ a ∧ y = Real.logb a (a / b) + Real.logb b (b / a) } := by

        Let's choose specific values for `a` and `b` that satisfy the conditions.
        
        Let `b = 2` and `a = 2`.
        Then `1 < 2` and `2 ≤ 2` are true.
        Now, compute `Real.logb 2 (2 / 2)` and `Real.logb 2 (2 / 2)`:
        - `Real.logb 2 (2 / 2) = Real.logb 2 1 = Real.log 1 / Real.log 2 = 0 / Real.log 2 = 0`
        - Similarly, `Real.logb 2 (2 / 2) = Real.logb 2 1 = 0`
        Thus, `Real.logb 2 (2 / 2) + Real.logb 2 (2 / 2) = 0 + 0 = 0`.
        Therefore, `0` is in the set.
        -/
        use 2, 2
        constructor
        · -- Prove 1 < 2
          norm_num
        · constructor
          · -- Prove 2 ≤ 2
            norm_num
          · -- Prove 0 = Real.logb 2 (2 / 2) + Real.logb 2 (2 / 2)
            have h₂ : Real.logb (2 : ℝ) (2 / 2) = 0 := by
              -- Prove that Real.logb 2 (2 / 2) = 0
              have h₃ : Real.logb (2 : ℝ) (2 / 2) = Real.log (2 / 2) / Real.log 2 := by
                rw [Real.logb]
              rw [h₃]
              have h₄ : Real.log (2 / 2) = 0 := by
                norm_num [Real.log_div, Real.log_one]
              rw [h₄]
              <;> simp [Real.logb]
            -- Combine the results to get the final equation
            have h₃ : Real.logb (2 : ℝ) (2 / 2) + Real.logb (2 : ℝ) (2 / 2) = 0 := by

            simpa using h₃

    · -- Show that 0 is the greatest element in the set

      have h₄ : y = Real.logb a (a / b) + Real.logb b (b / a) := by rw [h₃]

            rw [Real.logb]

          have h₅₇ : Real.log a > 0 := Real.log_pos (by linarith)
          have h₅₈ : Real.log b > 0 := Real.log_pos (by linarith)
          have h₅₉ : Real.log b ≤ Real.log a := by

            · linarith
          have h₅₁₀ : (Real.log a - Real.log b) / Real.log a + (Real.log b - Real.log a) / Real.log b ≤ 0 := by

          linarith

  have h₁ : (0 : ℝ) ∈ { y : ℝ | ∃ a b : ℝ, 1 < b ∧ b ≤ a ∧ y = Real.logb a (a / b) + Real.logb b (b / a) } := by

    Let's choose \(a = 2\) and \(b = 2\). 
    - First, we check \(1 < b\): \(1 < 2\) is true.
    - Next, we check \(b \leq a\): \(2 \leq 2\) is true.
    - We then compute \(y = \log_a(a/b) + \log_b(b/a)\):
      - \(\log_a(a/b) = \log_2(2/2) = \log_2(1) = 0\).
      - \(\log_b(b/a) = \log_2(2/2) = \log_2(1) = 0\).
      - Therefore, \(y = 0 + 0 = 0\).
    
    Since all conditions are satisfied, \(0\) is indeed a member of the set.
    -/
    refine' ⟨2, 2, by norm_num, by norm_num, _⟩
    have h₁ : Real.logb (2 : ℝ) ((2 : ℝ) / (2 : ℝ)) + Real.logb (2 : ℝ) ((2 : ℝ) / (2 : ℝ)) = 0 := by
      -- Prove that log_2(2/2) = 0
      have h₂ : Real.logb (2 : ℝ) ((2 : ℝ) / (2 : ℝ)) = 0 := by
        have h₃ : Real.logb (2 : ℝ) ((2 : ℝ) / (2 : ℝ)) = Real.log ((2 : ℝ) / (2 : ℝ)) / Real.log 2 := by
          rw [Real.logb]
          <;> field_simp
        rw [h₃]
        have h₄ : Real.log ((2 : ℝ) / (2 : ℝ)) = 0 := by
          have h₅ : (2 : ℝ) / (2 : ℝ) = 1 := by norm_num
          rw [h₅]
          have h₆ : Real.log 1 = 0 := by norm_num
          exact h₆
        rw [h₄]
        <;> field_simp
      -- Prove that log_2(2/2) + log_2(2/2) = 0
      linarith
    linarith
  
  have h₂ : ∀ y ∈ { y : ℝ | ∃ a b : ℝ, 1 < b ∧ b ≤ a ∧ y = Real.logb a (a / b) + Real.logb b (b / a) }, y ≤ 0 := by
    intro y hy

    rw [h₄]
    have h₅ : Real.logb a (a / b) + Real.logb b (b / a) ≤ 0 := by
      have h₆ : Real.logb a (a / b) = Real.log (a / b) / Real.log a := by
        rw [Real.logb]
      have h₇ : Real.logb b (b / a) = Real.log (b / a) / Real.log b := by
        rw [Real.logb]
      rw [h₆, h₇]
      have h₈ : Real.log (a / b) / Real.log a + Real.log (b / a) / Real.log b ≤ 0 := by
        have h₉ : Real.log a > 0 := by
          apply Real.log_pos
          linarith
        have h₁₀ : Real.log b > 0 := by
          apply Real.log_pos

        have h₁₁ : Real.log (a / b) = Real.log a - Real.log b := by
          rw [Real.log_div (by linarith) (by linarith)]
        have h₁₂ : Real.log (b / a) = Real.log b - Real.log a := by
          rw [Real.log_div (by linarith) (by linarith)]
        rw [h₁₁, h₁₂]
        have h₁₃ : ((Real.log a - Real.log b) / Real.log a + ((Real.log b - Real.log a) / Real.log b)) ≤ 0 := by

          have h₅₃₃ : a > 0 := by
            by_contra h'
            have h₅₃₄ : a ≤ 0 := by linarith
            have h₅₃₅ : b > 0 := by linarith
            have h₅₃₆ : a / b ≤ 0 := by
              apply div_nonpos_of_nonpos_of_nonneg
              · linarith
              · linarith
            linarith
          have h₅₃₄ : b > 0 := by linarith
          have h₅₃₅ : a / b ≤ 0 := by
            apply div_nonpos_of_nonneg_of_nonpos
            · linarith
            · linarith
          linarith
        have h₅₃₂ : Real.log (a / b) = Real.log a - Real.log b := by
          rw [Real.log_div (by linarith) (by linarith)]
          <;> ring_nf
        rw [h₅₃₂]
      have h₅₄ : Real.log (b / a) = Real.log b - Real.log a := by
        have h₅₄₁ : b / a > 0 := by
          by_contra h
          have h₅₄₂ : b / a ≤ 0 := by linarith
          have h₅₄₃ : b > 0 := by linarith
          have h₅₄₄ : a > 0 := by
            by_contra h'
            have h₅₄₅ : a ≤ 0 := by linarith
            have h₅₄₆ : a / b ≤ 0 := by
              apply div_nonpos_of_nonpos_of_nonneg
              · linarith
              · linarith
            have h₅₄₇ : a / b > 0 := by
              have h₅₄₈ : a / b > 0 := by
                apply div_pos
                · linarith
                · linarith
              linarith
            linarith
          have h₅₄₅ : b / a ≤ 0 := by
            apply div_nonpos_of_nonneg_of_nonpos
            · linarith
            · linarith
          linarith
        have h₅₄₂ : Real.log (b / a) = Real.log b - Real.log a := by
          rw [Real.log_div (by linarith) (by linarith)]
          <;> ring_nf
        rw [h₅₄₂]
      rw [h₅₃, h₅₄]
      have h₅₅ : Real.log a ≠ 0 := by
        have h₅₅₁ : a > 1 := by linarith
        have h₅₅₂ : Real.log a > 0 := Real.log_pos h₅₅₁
        linarith
      have h₅₆ : Real.log b ≠ 0 := by
        have h₅₆₁ : b > 1 := by linarith
        have h₅₆₂ : Real.log b > 0 := Real.log_pos h₅₆₁
        linarith
      field_simp [h₅₅, h₅₆]
      <;> ring_nf
      <;> field_simp [h₅₅, h₅₆]
      <;> ring_nf
      <;> linarith
    linarith
